[[Category:Cases]]
'''Possessive''' constructions in Aelaki express genitive relationships between nouns. Aelaki distinguishes two types of possession: '''inalienable''' and '''alienable''', each with its own morphological strategy.

== Inalienable Possession ==

Inalienable possession covers relationships that are inherent, permanent, or non-transferable:

* '''Kin relations''' (mother, father, sibling)
* '''Body parts''' (hand, eye, heart)
* '''Part-whole relationships''' (branch of a tree, wall of a house)

=== Formation ===

Inalienable possession uses an '''internal infix''' within the noun stem, following the pattern:

: C₁ + V(gender) + C(person) + ...

The possessive suffix ''-ng'' replaces the ''Ki'' suffix used in the [[Agent]] case.

=== Example ===

The noun '''gar''' ("hand"):

: '''gar''' → '''ga-o-r-ung''' ("my hand")

Here the root ''g-r'' is broken apart and the gender vowel ''o'' (feminine) and person consonant are infixed, with ''-ung'' as the possessive ending.

== Alienable Possession ==

Alienable possession covers relationships that are transferable or abstract:

* '''Transferable property''' (books, tools, clothing)
* '''Abstract relations''' (ideas, responsibilities)

=== Formation ===

Alienable possession uses a '''prefix''' before the noun, following the pattern:

: V(gender) + C(person)- + noun

=== Example ===

The noun '''selo''' ("book"):

: '''selo''' → '''uthselo''' ("my book")

Here the gender vowel and person consonant are prefixed to the noun stem.

== Case Hierarchy ==

Possessive ranks between [[Instrumental]] and [[Speaker]] in the case hierarchy for verb agreement:

[[Agent]] > [[Dative]] > [[Patient]] > [[Instrumental]] > '''Possessive''' > [[Speaker]]

== See Also ==

* [[Agent]] - marks the subject of transitive/active verbs
* [[Patient]] - marks the object of transitive verbs
* [[Instrumental]] - marks the instrument, companion, or location
* [[Dative]] - marks the recipient or beneficiary
* [[Speaker]] - marks the source of reported information

[[Category:git synced pages]]
